name: Academy Award for Best International Feature Film (formerly Best Foreign Language Film)

wins.title: The Virgin Spring
wins.plotSummary: In medieval Sweden, devout farmer Töre sends his cherished daughter Karin to deliver candles to a distant church, accompanied by her resentful, pregnant servant Ingeri, who secretly still worships the old pagan gods. Alone in the forest, Karin is raped and murdered by a trio of goat herders, who unknowingly seek shelter that same night at her family's farm and try to sell her clothes to her own mother. Ingmar Bergman's stark tragedy follows Töre's brutal vengeance against the killers and his reckoning with his own faith when a spring miraculously wells up where his daughter died.

wins.title: Through a Glass Darkly
wins.plotSummary: Over a single day on a remote Swedish island, Karin, recently released from a mental hospital, spends time with her husband, her emotionally distant novelist father, and her frustrated teenage brother, all four unable to truly reach one another. As Karin's schizophrenia worsens and she becomes convinced God is speaking to her through the walls, her father is torn between his need to observe her breakdown for his writing and his guilt over his own emotional absence from the family. Ingmar Bergman's chamber drama closes with a fragile, uncertain image of divine love found only in the family's small, human gestures toward each other.

wins.title: Sundays and Cybèle
wins.originalTitle: Cybèle ou Les dimanches de Ville d'Avray
wins.plotSummary: Pierre, a former pilot left amnesiac and childlike after a plane crash in the French Indochina War, struggles to return the romantic devotion of Madeleine, the nurse he now lives with. He befriends Françoise, an 11-year-old girl abandoned at a boarding school by her father, and the two form an innocent, secretive friendship, meeting every Sunday for walks through the park while Pierre poses as her father. Serge Bourguignon's delicate drama follows their bond toward a tragic misunderstanding, as the adult world reads sinister motives into what the two damaged souls experience as pure companionship.

wins.title: 8½
wins.plotSummary: Guido Anselmi, a celebrated Italian film director, retreats to a spa town suffering from creative paralysis on his ambitious new science-fiction film, unable to reconcile the competing demands of his producers, mistress, wife, and his own fading inspiration. As pressure mounts and his film's massive, half-built rocket-launch set sits unused, Guido drifts between memory, fantasy, and reality, replaying childhood memories and imagined conversations with the women who have shaped his life. Federico Fellini's self-reflexive masterpiece turns Guido's inability to make his film into the very film itself, ending in a surreal circus parade of everyone in his life.

wins.title: Yesterday, Today and Tomorrow
wins.plotSummary: Three loosely connected stories, each starring Sophia Loren and Marcello Mastroianni, follow couples across different regions of Italy: a poor Neapolitan black-market cigarette seller who exploits a legal loophole protecting pregnant women from prison by staying perpetually pregnant; a bored, wealthy Milanese housewife carrying on an affair with a lower-class writer; and a wealthy Roman call girl whose favorite client is a besotted young seminary student. Vittorio De Sica's anthology comedy uses each vignette to explore a different shade of Italian womanhood and desire, anchored by Loren and Mastroianni's shifting chemistry across all three, from social comedy to farce.

wins.title: The Shop on Main Street
wins.plotSummary: During World War II in the Nazi-aligned Slovak Republic, mild-mannered carpenter Tóno Brtko is appointed "Aryan controller" of a small notions shop owned by elderly, nearly deaf Jewish widow Rozália Lautmannová, who barely understands what is happening to her. Tóno, unable to bring himself to exploit her, instead quietly supports the failing shop himself while hiding the truth of the Aryanization program from her. When Slovak Jews are ordered deported, Tóno faces an impossible choice between saving Rozália and protecting himself, with catastrophic consequences either way.

wins.title: A Man and a Woman
wins.plotSummary: Anne, a widowed script supervisor still grieving her stuntman husband's death on a film set, and Jean-Louis, a widowed race car driver whose wife died by suicide after his near-fatal crash, meet by chance while visiting their children at the same boarding school. Drawn together by shared loss and quiet recognition, they begin a tentative romance shadowed by the memory of the spouses they can't quite let go of. Claude Lelouch's romance, shifting fluidly between color, black-and-white, and sepia, follows the couple's hesitant attempt to build something new out of parallel grief.

wins.title: Closely Watched Trains
wins.plotSummary: Miloš Hrma, a young apprentice train dispatcher at a small station in German-occupied Czechoslovakia, is more preoccupied with losing his virginity to his girlfriend Máša and enduring his colleagues' teasing than with the war raging around him. His sexual anxieties and a botched suicide attempt play out alongside the station staff's petty rivalries, until a chance to strike a real blow against a German munitions train forces Miloš into an act of quiet, decisive courage. Jiří Menzel's bittersweet coming-of-age comedy turns wartime resistance into something achieved almost by accident, undercutting heroism with everyday awkwardness right up to its sudden final act.

wins.title: War and Peace
wins.plotSummary: Spanning Napoleon's invasion of Russia, the film follows illegitimate nobleman's son Pierre Bezukhov, idealistic Prince Andrei Bolkonsky, and the vivacious young Natasha Rostova as their lives intertwine through Russian high society, war, love, and loss. Andrei goes to war against Napoleon and is presumed dead, Natasha's engagement to him collapses after a near-elopement with another man, and Pierre's own disastrous marriage leaves him searching for meaning amid Russia's mounting national crisis. Sergei Bondarchuk's four-part epic, filmed with tens of thousands of Soviet Army extras, builds to Moscow's occupation and the survivors' hard-won reckoning with what the war has cost them.

wins.title: Z
studios: Office National pour le Commerce et l'Industrie Cinématographique
wins.plotSummary: In an unnamed Mediterranean country modeled on 1960s Greece, a leftist deputy is fatally struck down by right-wing thugs after a political rally, in what authorities immediately rule an accident. A dogged young magistrate, initially expected to rubber-stamp the cover-up, instead digs into the case and uncovers a conspiracy reaching into the highest levels of the military and police. Costa-Gavras's furious political thriller follows the investigation's inconvenient triumph, only for a bitter epilogue to reveal how thoroughly the guilty escape consequences once the ruling junta reasserts control.

wins.title: Investigation of a Citizen Above Suspicion
wins.originalTitle: Indagine su un cittadino al di sopra di ogni sospetto
wins.plotSummary: A recently promoted Roman police inspector murders his mistress and then inserts himself into the investigation of his own crime, deliberately planting obvious clues while steering suspicion toward other suspects, including the victim's husband and a student radical. Convinced his authority makes him untouchable, he grows increasingly reckless, all but daring his subordinates to arrest him as a way of proving his power is absolute. Elio Petri's satire follows his mounting self-destructive arrogance as a portrait of institutional corruption immune to consequence.

wins.title: The Garden of the Finzi-Continis
wins.plotSummary: In 1938 Ferrara, the wealthy Jewish Finzi-Contini family opens their walled estate to friends of their children Micòl and Alberto after Jews are banned from the city's tennis club. Giorgio, a Jewish businessman's son, has loved Micòl since adolescence, but she stays distant and eventually takes up with his gentile friend Giampiero instead. As Mussolini's racial laws tighten and war approaches, Giorgio drifts away from the family while Micòl clings to their insulated privilege. When Italy's Jews are rounded up in 1943, the Finzi-Continis are arrested together, and a last fleeting reunion between Giorgio and Micòl ends with their fates left uncertain.

wins.title: The Discreet Charm of the Bourgeoisie
wins.originalTitle: Le Charme discret de la bourgeoisie
wins.plotSummary: A group of French bourgeois friends and the ambassador of a fictional South American country repeatedly try, and repeatedly fail, to sit down together for a proper dinner, thwarted each time by absurd interruptions ranging from a restaurant owner's wake to a full military ambush. Luis Buñuel interweaves these thwarted gatherings with surreal dream sequences from various characters, blurring the line between the group's waking rituals and their subconscious anxieties. The film never resolves into a single finished dinner, instead using the characters' endless, oblivious return to social ritual as a satire of bourgeois complacency.

wins.title: Day for Night
wins.plotSummary: A film crew struggles through the chaotic production of a mediocre melodrama called "Meet Pamela," juggling an aging leading man, a fragile Italian star, a volatile young heartthrob, and a British actress recovering from a breakdown. François Truffaut, who also plays the beleaguered director Ferrand, weaves together the on- and off-set entanglements of cast and crew into a warmly self-aware love letter to the compromises and small miracles of filmmaking itself. The film's title refers to the technique of shooting night scenes in daylight, a fitting metaphor for the illusion-making at its core.

wins.title: Amarcord
wins.plotSummary: In the seaside village of Borgo San Giuliano during 1930s Fascist Italy, adolescent Titta comes of age amid a parade of eccentric neighbors, family squabbles, and communal rituals, from a springtime bonfire to a fog-shrouded ocean-liner sighting. Federico Fellini's loosely autobiographical film moves through the changing seasons of a single year, mixing broad comedy with wistful nostalgia as it captures the town's collective sexual fantasies, provincial pretensions, and susceptibility to Mussolini's posturing. Held together less by plot than by memory, the film builds an affectionate, satirical portrait of a community too comfortable in its own illusions to grow up.

wins.title: Dersu Uzala
wins.plotSummary: In the early 20th century, Russian explorer Vladimir Arsenyev leads survey expeditions into the Siberian wilderness and forms a deep bond with Dersu Uzala, a native trapper whose intimate knowledge of the taiga repeatedly saves the expedition's lives. As their friendship deepens over years of shared hardship, Arsenyev comes to see Dersu not as a guide but as a wise equal, while Dersu's failing eyesight and inability to adapt to city life set up the story's melancholy final act. Akira Kurosawa's Soviet co-production frames their friendship as a meditation on nature, aging, and the incompatibility of two ways of life.

wins.title: Black and White in Color
wins.plotSummary: In 1915, a small, complacent French colonial outpost in Cameroon is jolted out of its leisurely isolation by news that France is at war with Germany, whose colony sits just across the border. Eager to prove their patriotism despite having no real stake in the European conflict, the settlers organize a bumbling, ill-equipped military campaign against their German neighbors, escalating a comfortable rivalry into real violence. Jean-Jacques Annaud's directorial debut plays the colonists' warmongering as farce that curdles into a sharp indictment of colonial arrogance on both sides.

wins.title: Madame Rosa
wins.plotSummary: In the Belleville neighborhood of Paris, elderly Holocaust survivor and former prostitute Madame Rosa runs an informal boarding house for the children of sex workers, including Momo, an Algerian boy she's raised as her own despite hiding his true age. As Rosa's health declines, Momo, initially a troublemaker, comes to understand and care for her more deeply, discovering the hidden Jewish identity and haunted past she's kept concealed even from him. The film follows their unconventional found-family bond as Rosa's life draws to a close, with Momo determined to care for her himself rather than let her be taken away.

wins.title: Get Out Your Handkerchiefs
wins.plotSummary: Worried about his wife Solange's unexplained depression, Raoul recruits another man, Stéphane, to become her lover as well, hoping a change might lift her spirits, but neither man's attention cures what ails her. When the trio takes summer jobs at a boys' camp, Solange becomes fixated on Christian, a bullied 13-year-old math prodigy, and begins an affair with him that unsettles even the two men who orchestrated her earlier arrangement. Bertrand Blier's provocative comedy follows Solange's search for fulfillment through increasingly transgressive relationships that none of the men around her can fully understand or contain.

wins.title: The Tin Drum
wins.plotSummary: In the Free City of Danzig before and during World War II, Oskar Matzerath, a child of uncertain parentage born to a woman torn between her German husband and Polish lover, wills himself to stop growing at age three rather than join the adult world. Armed with a tin drum he beats furiously whenever displeased and a scream that can shatter glass, Oskar becomes a mocking, unsettling witness to his family's petty betrayals and the rise of Nazism around him. Volker Schlöndorff's adaptation of Günter Grass's novel uses Oskar's arrested body and clear-eyed cruelty to view the era's horrors from the perspective of someone who refuses, in every sense, to grow up.

wins.title: Moscow Does Not Believe in Tears
wins.plotSummary: In 1958 Moscow, three young women share a dormitory while chasing different visions of happiness: factory worker Katerina, marriage-minded Lyudmila, and the more grounded Antonina. Katerina falls for a television cameraman who fathers her child and then abandons her, leaving her to raise their daughter alone while building a career. Twenty years later, now a successful factory director, she meets a warm-hearted metalworker named Gosha but conceals her status from him, and when the truth comes out it nearly derails their romance before a hard-won reconciliation.

wins.title: Mephisto
wins.plotSummary: Ambitious actor Hendrik Höfgen claws his way up from provincial German theaters to national stardom, most famously playing Mephistopheles in a production of Faust. When the Nazis seize power, he chooses to stay and align himself with the new regime rather than flee like his colleagues, using his old leftist ties as leverage for forgiveness and advancement. His fame grows alongside his moral compromises, until he comes to embody the very devil he plays on stage, propped up by patrons far more dangerous than himself.

wins.title: Begin the Beguine
wins.plotSummary: Antonio Albajar, an aging writer newly awarded the Nobel Prize, returns to his hometown of Gijón, Spain after decades in exile abroad. There he reconnects with old friends and rekindles a long-dormant love with Elena, a woman he left behind years earlier. Their reunion stirs up memories of Spain's turbulent past under Franco and gives Antonio a chance to make peace with the choices that shaped his life.

wins.title: Fanny and Alexander
wins.plotSummary: In early-1900s Uppsala, siblings Fanny and Alexander live a rich theatrical family life until their father dies and their mother remarries a severe, authoritarian bishop. The children are moved into his household, where Alexander's vivid imagination and storytelling bring him cruel punishment, and the family becomes desperate to free them. A rescue is staged with the help of relatives, culminating in a fire that kills the bishop, after which the family reunites and Alexander is left haunted by visions of the man who tormented him.

wins.title: Dangerous Moves
wins.plotSummary: At a world chess championship in Geneva, an aging Soviet grandmaster with a failing heart faces off against a younger rival who defected from the USSR years earlier. As the match wears on, both men are worn down by forces beyond the board: the Soviet delegation's political pressure and surveillance, and the defector's own crumbling personal life. Their contest becomes as much a psychological and ideological battle as a game of chess, with neither side able to claim an easy victory.

wins.title: The Official Story
wins.plotSummary: In Buenos Aires in 1983, history teacher Alicia lives a comfortable life with her government-connected husband Roberto and their adopted daughter Gaby, largely insulated from her country's recent political violence. Conversations with a returning friend and a chance meeting with a grandmother searching for a missing grandchild lead Alicia to suspect that Gaby was taken from a mother who disappeared under the military dictatorship. As she pushes for the truth, Roberto's evasiveness and anger force Alicia to confront both her husband's complicity and her own willful blindness, and she ultimately leaves him.

wins.title: The Assault
wins.plotSummary: In January 1945, near the end of the Nazi occupation of the Netherlands, a collaborator is assassinated outside the Steenwijk family's home, and neighbors move his body in front of their door to shift blame. In retaliation, the Germans kill Anton Steenwijk's parents and brother and take the boy into custody. Decades later, in the postwar years and again in the 1980s, an adult Anton keeps circling back to that night, piecing together what really happened and who bears responsibility for his family's death.

wins.title: Babette's Feast
wins.plotSummary: In a remote, devout Danish coastal village, elderly sisters Martine and Filippa have spent their lives tending their late father's austere religious community, having each given up a chance at love and a life beyond the village. Babette, a French refugee fleeing political turmoil, arrives and works as their housekeeper for fourteen years. After winning a lottery, she spends her entire winnings on an elaborate French feast for the congregation, and the meal's sensuous generosity quietly overwhelms the villagers' guarded piety, revealing Babette to have once been a celebrated Parisian chef.

wins.title: Pelle the Conqueror
wins.plotSummary: In the late 19th century, elderly Swedish widower Lasse and his young son Pelle emigrate to the Danish island of Bornholm in search of work, ending up as poorly treated laborers on a large farm. Pelle slowly adapts to his new home, learning the language and enduring the prejudice of his Danish peers while dreaming, like a fellow farmhand, of a bigger life beyond the island. As the farm's own domestic dramas unfold around them and Lasse's brief romance collapses, Lasse eventually urges his son to leave and chase the future for himself.

wins.title: Cinema Paradiso
wins.plotSummary: In 1988 Rome, Salvatore Di Vita, a famous film director, returns home late one evening, where his girlfriend sleepily tells him that his mother called to say someone named Alfredo has died. Salvatore shies from committed relationships and has not been to his home village of Giancaldo, Sicily, in thirty years. As his girlfriend asks him who Alfredo was, Salvatore is unable to fall asleep and flashes back to his childhood.

wins.title: Journey of Hope
wins.plotSummary: The film begins in a remote and impoverished village in Eastern Turkey, where the Öztürk family resides. They consist of the father, Haydar; the mother, Meryem; and their seven children, including their young son, Mehmet. Haydar, a hardworking farmer, is struggling to provide for his family amidst political turmoil and economic difficulties.

wins.title: Mediterraneo
wins.plotSummary: In 1941, one year after Italy joined Germany against the Allies in World War II, a small group of misfit Italian soldiers is sent to Megísti, a small Greek island in the Aegean Sea, for four months of lookout duty. The soldiers include a lieutenant who likes art, a macho sergeant, a ski instructor accompanied by his beloved donkey Silvana, and other quirky people. They are not very good soldiers, but a cross section of average, independent men.

wins.title: Indochine
wins.plotSummary: In 1930, Éliane Devries, a woman born to French parents in colonial Indochina, runs her and her widowed father's rubber plantation with indentured laborers. She is the adoptive mother of Camille, a teenaged girl whose birth parents were friends of Éliane's and members of the Nguyễn dynasty. Éliane meets young French Navy lieutenant Jean-Baptiste Le Guen when they bid on the same painting at an auction.

wins.title: Belle Époque
wins.plotSummary: In February 1931, some weeks after the failed Jaca uprising and the likewise failed Cuatro Vientos uprising, Spain is on the verge of the proclamation of the Second Republic. Fernando, a deserting private with Republican leanings and former seminarist, is on the run from his assignment at the Cuatro Vientos base. After escaping from two Guardia Civil officers, he reaches the outskirts of a village, befriending Manolo, an old man with a semblance of a "Dickensian observer of life".

wins.title: Burnt by the Sun
wins.plotSummary: The entirety of the film takes place within the course of one day in the summer of 1936 in the Soviet Union. Mitya (Dmitri), a former nobleman and veteran of the anti-communist White Army, contemplates suicide. The film cuts to Komdiv Sergei Petrovich Kotov, his wife Maroussia, and their young six-year-old daughter Nadia relaxing in a banya (sauna) when a peasant from the local collective farm frantically tells them the Red Army's tanks are about to crush the wheat harvest as part of general maneuvers.

wins.title: Antonia's Line
wins.plotSummary: Following World War II, the widow Antonia and her daughter Danielle arrive in Antonia's home town where her mother is dying. She reunites with her old friend Crooked Finger, a depressed intellectual who refuses to leave his house. She also begins attracting a following, including Deedee, a mentally handicapped girl, after she is raped by her brother Pitte, and Loony Lips, a simple-minded man who falls in love with Deedee.

wins.title: Kolya
wins.plotSummary: The film begins in 1988 in Soviet-occupied Czechoslovakia. František Louka, a middle-aged man dedicated to bachelorhood and the pursuit of women, is a concert cellist struggling to eke out a living by playing funerals at the Prague crematoriums. He has lost his previous job at the Czech Philharmonic, having been blacklisted as "politically unreliable" by the authorities.

wins.title: Character
wins.plotSummary: In 1930's Rotterdam, A.B. Dreverhaven, a dreaded bailiff, is found dead, with a knife stuck in his stomach. The obvious suspect is Jacob Willem Katadreuffe, an ambitious young lawyer who worked his way up from poverty, always managing to overcome Dreverhaven's personal attacks against him.

wins.title: Life Is Beautiful
wins.plotSummary: In 1939, in Fascist Italy, young Italian Jew Guido Orefice arrives to work in Arezzo, Tuscany, with his uncle Eliseo in a hotel restaurant. He is comical and sharp, and falls in love with the gentile girl Dora. Later, Guido sees her again in the city where she is a teacher and set to be engaged to Rodolfo, a rich but arrogant local government official with whom he regularly clashes.

wins.title: All About My Mother
wins.plotSummary: Manuela is an Argentine nurse who supervises donor organ transplants at Ramón y Cajal Hospital in Madrid. She is also a single mother to Esteban, a teenager who aspires to become a writer. On the night of Esteban's 17th birthday, he is hit and killed by a car while chasing after his favorite actress, Huma Rojo, for her autograph following a performance of A Streetcar Named Desire, where Huma portrays Blanche DuBois.

wins.title: Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon
wins.plotSummary: In 1778, Qing dynasty China, Li Mu Bai is a renowned Wudang swordsman, and his friend Yu Shu Lien heads a private security company. Shu Lien and Mu Bai have long had feelings for each other, but because Shu Lien had been engaged to Mu Bai's close friend Meng Sizhao before his death, Shu Lien and Mu Bai feel bound by loyalty to Meng Sizhao and have not revealed their feelings to each other. Mu Bai, choosing to retire from the life of a swordsman, asks Shu Lien to give his fabled 400-year-old jian sword "Green Destiny" to their benefactor Sir Te in Beijing.

wins.title: No Man's Land
wins.plotSummary: During the Bosnian War, two wounded soldiers, a Bosniak named Čiki and a Bosnian Serb named Nino, are caught between their lines in no man's land. The two soldiers confront each other in a trench, where they wait for dark. Trading insults at first, they eventually begin to find some common ground.

wins.title: Nowhere in Africa
wins.plotSummary: In 1938, the Redlich family flees to Kenya from Leobschütz in Silesia, Nazi Germany, to escape the increasing persecution of the Jews. Walter, a former lawyer, finds work as a farm manager and sends for his family. His wife Jettel has trouble adjusting to life in Africa, although their daughter Regina quickly adapts to her new environment, easily learning the language of the country and showing interest in local culture.

wins.title: The Barbarian Invasions
wins.plotSummary: Seventeen years after the events of The Decline of the American Empire, Sébastien is enjoying a successful career in quantitative finance in London when he receives a call from his mother, Louise, that his father and Louise's ex-husband Rémy is terminally ill with cancer. Sébastien is not enthused about seeing Rémy, whom he blames for breaking up the family with his many adulteries. Rémy and his friends of the older generation are still largely social-democrats and proponents of Quebec nationalism.

wins.title: The Sea Inside
wins.plotSummary: This is the life story of Spaniard Ramón Sampedro, who fought a 28-year campaign to win the right to end his own life with assisted suicide. The film explores Ramón's relationships with two women: Julia, a lawyer suffering from Cadasil syndrome who supports his cause, and Rosa, a local woman who wants to convince him that his life is worth living. Through the gift of his love, these two women are inspired to accomplish things they never previously thought possible.

wins.title: Tsotsi
studios: The UK Film & TV Production Company PLC
Industrial Development Corporation of South Africa
The National Film and Video Foundation of SA
wins.plotSummary: As his mother is dying of disease, David runs away from an abusive father and lives with other homeless children in a series of large concrete construction pipes. A few years later, David, who now goes by Tsotsi, is the leader of a gang that includes his friends Butcher, Aap, and Boston. After getting involved in a murder committed by Butcher during a mugging, Tsotsi and Boston get into a fight that leaves Boston badly injured.

wins.title: The Lives of Others
wins.plotSummary: In 1984 East Germany (GDR), Stasi Hauptmann Gerd Wiesler, code name HGW XX/7, is ordered by his friend and superior, Lt. Col. Anton Grubitz, to spy on playwright Georg Dreyman, whose pro-communist politics and international recognition have so far kept the state from directly monitoring him. Dreyman's apparent life as a model East German mystifies Wiesler; the playwright has no known vices or record of disloyalty or dissent at all. At the request of Minister of Culture Bruno Hempf, Wiesler and his team bug Dreyman's apartment, set up surveillance equipment, and report Dreyman's activities.

wins.title: The Counterfeiters
wins.plotSummary: The film begins shortly after the end of the Second World War, with a man arriving in Monte Carlo. After checking into an expensive hotel and paying with cash, he takes in the high life of Monte Carlo, successfully gambling in a casino and attracting the attention of a beautiful French woman. Later, she discovers tattooed numerals on his arm, revealing him as a survivor of the Nazi concentration camps.

wins.title: Departures
wins.plotSummary: Daigo Kobayashi loses his job as a cellist when his orchestra is disbanded. He and his wife Mika move from Tokyo to his hometown in Yamagata, where they live in his childhood home that was left to him when his mother died two years earlier. It is fronted by a coffee shop that Daigo's father had operated before he ran off with a waitress when Daigo was six; since then the two have had no contact.

wins.title: The Secret in Their Eyes
wins.plotSummary: In June 1974, judiciary agent Benjamín Espósito investigates the rape and murder of Liliana Colotto. Espósito promises her husband, Ricardo Morales, that he will find the killer and ensure a life sentence. Espósito is assisted by his alcoholic partner Pablo Sandoval and the new department chief, Irene Menéndez-Hastings.

wins.title: In a Better World
wins.plotSummary: Anton (Mikael Persbrandt) is a Swedish doctor who commutes between his home in Denmark and his work in a Sudanese refugee camp. In Sudan, he often treats female patients who are the victims of a sadistic warlord. Anton is married to Marianne (Trine Dyrholm), but they are separated, and struggling with the possibility of divorce over an affair that Anton had with another woman.

wins.title: A Separation
wins.plotSummary: Nader, Simin, and their 10-year-old daughter, Termeh, are an upper-middle-class family living in a flat in Tehran. Simin wants the whole family to leave Iran and has prepared the visas, but Nader wishes to stay to care for his father, who lives with them, and has Alzheimer's disease. Simin therefore files for divorce, but the family court considers the grounds to be insufficient and rejects the application.

wins.title: Amour
wins.plotSummary: After residents of a Paris apartment building complain of a smell coming from one of the apartments, the emergency services break down its door to find an elderly woman's corpse in the bedroom, adorned with cut flowers. Several months before the opening scene, Anne and her husband Georges, both retired piano teachers in their eighties, attend a performance by one of Anne's former pupils, Alexandre. They return home to find that someone has unsuccessfully tried to break into their apartment.

wins.title: The Great Beauty
wins.plotSummary: Jep Gambardella is a 65-year-old seasoned journalist and theater critic, mostly committed to wandering among the social events of a Rome immersed in the beauty of its history and in the superficiality of its inhabitants today, in a merciless contrast. He also ventured into creative writing in his youth: he is the author of only one work called The Human Apparatus. Despite the appreciation and the many awards he received, Jep has not written other books, not only for his laziness but above all for a creative block from which he cannot escape.

wins.title: Ida
wins.plotSummary: In the 1960s Poland, Anna, a young novice nun, is told by her prioress that she must visit Wanda Gruz, her aunt and only surviving relative, before she takes her vows. Anna travels to Warsaw to see Wanda, a chain-smoking, hard-drinking, sexually promiscuous state prosecutor and former communist resistance fighter. Wanda reveals that Anna's actual name is Ida Lebenstein, and that she was raised in a convent after her Jewish parents were murdered late in the German occupation during World War II.

wins.title: Son of Saul
wins.plotSummary: In October 1944, Jewish–Hungarian prisoner Saul Ausländer works as a Sonderkommando at the gas chambers in Auschwitz. He works stoically, seemingly numbed by the daily atrocities. Among the dead after a gassing, Saul sees a still living boy suffocated by a Nazi doctor who calls for an autopsy.

wins.title: The Salesman
wins.plotSummary: Emad and Rana are a married couple who both work in the theatre, currently starring in a production of Death of a Salesman by Arthur Miller, with Emad portraying Willy Loman and Rana playing Linda. Emad is also a popular instructor at a local school, where the youth joke about him being a "salesman." One night, their apartment begins to collapse and they flee the building with the other residents.

wins.title: A Fantastic Woman
wins.plotSummary: Marina is a young trans woman living in Santiago, Chile. She works as a singer and a waitress and is in a relationship with an older man named Orlando. They have recently moved in together, and on Marina's birthday, Orlando gives her a note promising tickets to a famous resort as a gift.

wins.title: Roma
wins.plotSummary: In 1970, Cleodegaria "Cleo" Gutiérrez is a Mixtec live-in maid in an upper-middle-class household in Mexico City's Colonia Roma neighborhood. The household consists of the mother, Sofía; the father, Antonio; their four school-aged children, Pepe, Sofi, Toño and Paco; and Sofía's mother, Teresa. Antonio, a medical doctor, often leaves for business conferences, but Sofía's distressed reactions to his absences suggest he is having an extramarital affair.

wins.title: Parasite
wins.plotSummary: Kim Ki-taek and Chung-sook live in a semi-basement flat (banjiha) in Seoul with their daughter Ki-jung and son Ki-woo. As the family has financial struggles and low-income jobs, family friend Min-hyuk gives them a scholar's rock meant to promise wealth. Leaving to study abroad, he suggests that Ki-woo lie about his qualifications to take over Min-hyuk's job as an English tutor for Da-hye, the daughter of the wealthy but naïve Park family.

wins.title: Another Round
wins.plotSummary: Teachers Martin, Tommy, Peter, and Nikolaj are colleagues and friends who work at a gymnasium school in Copenhagen. All four struggle with unmotivated students and feel that their lives have become boring and stale. Martin is confronted by his senior students and their parents, who express that he has become a barrier to their passing their history exams.

wins.title: Drive My Car
wins.originalTitle: ドライブ・マイ・カー
wins.plotSummary: Stage actor and director Yūsuke Kafuku lives in Tokyo with his wife Oto, a screenwriter who conceives her stories after sex and narrates them to him. He learns his lines by listening to tapes recorded by Oto, while driving his red Aero performance variant of Saab 900 Turbo. After Yūsuke performs in Waiting for Godot, Oto introduces him to young television star Kōji Takatsuki.

wins.title: All Quiet on the Western Front
wins.plotSummary: In 1917, three years into World War I, 17-year-old Paul Bäumer enthusiastically enlists in the Imperial German Army alongside schoolmates Albert Kropp, Franz Müller, and Ludwig Behm. They hear a patriotic speech by a school official and unknowingly receive recycled uniforms from soldiers killed in a previous battle. After they are deployed in northern France near La Malmaison, they are befriended by an older and more experienced soldier, Stanislaus "Kat" Katczinsky.

wins.title: The Zone of Interest
wins.plotSummary: In 1943, Rudolf Höss, commandant of the German Auschwitz concentration camp, lives with his wife Hedwig and their five children in an idyllic home next to the camp. Höss takes the children out to swim and fish, and Hedwig spends time tending the garden. Non-Jewish inmates handle the chores, and the murdered Jews' best belongings are given to the family.

wins.title: I'm Still Here
wins.plotSummary: In December 1970, former Brazilian congressman Rubens Paiva lives in a house near Leblon beach in Rio de Janeiro with his wife Eunice and their five children. Returning to his civil career, after the revocation of his tenure as a congressman following the 1964 Brazilian coup d'état, Paiva continues to support political expatriates without disclosing his activities to his family. After revolutionary movements kidnap the Swiss ambassador to Brazil, the country faces looming political instability.
